symptoms.symptom_name: Excessive heart racing (palpitations) on sitting up or standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excessive posture-dependent tachycardia is the cardinal POTS symptom.
Palpitations require exclusion of a primary arrhythmia, but a strictly postural trigger argues away from a fixed arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms resolve within ~1 minute of lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Posture-dependence with rapid resolution on recumbency is the discriminating feature of orthostatic intolerance.
A primary tachyarrhythmia would not reliably terminate simply on lying flat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pre-syncope: tunnel vision, dizziness and leg weakness on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cerebral hypoperfusion on standing produces posture-dependent pre-syncope.
Pre-syncope is shared with vasovagal events; the sustained tachycardia favours POTS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-collapse without true loss of consciousness or injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-syncope (not syncope) fits severe orthostatic intolerance and drives fall risk.
The absence of true loss of consciousness lowers the probability of an arrhythmic syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain and no palpitations at rest (heart rate normal lying still)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal heart rate at rest with no resting palpitations argues strongly against a sustained primary arrhythmia.
Absence of chest pain lowers ACS and PE probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-viral background with chronic postural dizziness and profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-viral onset of chronic orthostatic intolerance and fatigue is the characteristic context for post-viral dysautonomia / Long COVID POTS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tolerating oral fluids and passing urine; not vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maintained oral intake and urine output argue against severe volume depletion as the driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active-stand postural vitals: HR 72→128 bpm (+56), BP 115/75→110/75 (no orthostatic drop)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician active-stand measurement: excessive HR increment WITHOUT a BP fall is the POTS-pattern signature. The AI Doctor cannot obtain this remotely and infers the pattern from the posture-dependent symptoms; formal confirmation is outpatient autonomic testing.
Blood pressure is maintained on standing — excludes orthostatic hypotension as the mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular cardiac rhythm without murmurs; clear chest; normal oxygen saturation (SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person exam: regular rhythm without murmur lowers structural/arrhythmic probability (a resting ECG is still the appropriate outpatient investigation).
Clear chest and normal oxygen saturation argue against PE.
Sealed in-person finding: moist membranes exclude clinically severe dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting 12-lead ECG (to exclude a primary arrhythmia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A resting ECG is the appropriate outpatient investigation to exclude a primary arrhythmia; it cannot be obtained via telehealth and, given the negative acute screen, is arranged as prompt outpatient workup rather than emergently.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of excessive posture-dependent tachycardia, pre-syncope that resolves on lying flat, no chest pain and a normal resting heart rate, maintained hydration, and a post-viral background — with a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen — identifies a benign chronic dysautonomia flare appropriate for prompt outpatient management, not emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate outpatient investigation to exclude a primary arrhythmia before attributing symptoms fully to dysautonomia. Cannot be done via telehealth; arrange at the prompt GP review — not emergently, given the negative acute screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Active-stand (lying-and-standing) blood pressure and heart rate / formal autonomic testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the POTS pattern (HR increment without BP fall). Bedside active-stand at the GP; head-up tilt if needed via specialist.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (FBC, electrolytes, iron studies, TSH, morning cortisol as indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for anaemia, electrolyte disturbance, thyroid dysfunction and other contributors to orthostatic intolerance and fatigue as outpatient workup.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency same-day ECG + troponin + D-dimer as the default pathway
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated as the baseline disposition: the acute red-flag screen is negative (postural-only, resolves lying flat, no chest pain, no supine tachycardia, tolerating fluids). Routing every such flare to an emergency cardiac/PE workup is the over-triage this case is designed to detect — unless a genuine escalation trigger appears.
